user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear bumper area and surrounding body are clearly visible in img_1, allowing assessment of deformation and damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows severe deformation of the trunk lid, a missing rear bumper cover with exposed inner structure showing denting and rust, and misaligned taillights, consistent with the user's claim of new damage to the rear bumper area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images show the front bumper area clearly enough to assess damage, but the two images show different vehicles and neither matches the claimed minor scratch.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a different car with severe collision damage (not a light scratch), and img_2 shows a pristine white sedan with no visible damage on the front bumper. Neither image supports the claim of a light scratch on the front bumper.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows a large spiderweb crack on the windshield, which is relevant to the claim. Image 2 shows an intact windshield, creating a mismatch.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 shows a heavily cracked windshield consistent with the claim, but Image 2 shows an entirely intact windshield of the same part, creating a contradiction between the submitted images. The mismatch between images undermines the claim's reliability.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The side mirror is clearly visible in img_1 with cracked glass and damaged housing, sufficient to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the side mirror with cracked glass and damaged housing, consistent with the user's claim of mirror damage.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows the rear bumper area of a car with a visible dent; img_2 shows the full rear end with no visible damage. The relevant part is visible across the set, though img_1 is cropped.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a clear dent on the rear bumper/quarter panel area of a silver car, consistent with the customer's claim of rear bumper damage. img_2 shows the same rear end without visible damage, but the dent is documented in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows the side/rear door area of the car; the headlight is not visible, so the claimed damage cannot be inspected.
claim_status_justification: img_1 does not show the headlight; the claimed part is not visible in the submitted image, so there is insufficient evidence to support or contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 2 clearly shows the claimed dent on the door panel; Image 1 is blurry but Image 2 alone meets the evidence standard.
claim_status_justification: Image 2 shows a dent with scratches on the lower door panel near the seam, matching the user's claim of a dent on the door panel area.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a car with the hood visible, but the claimed scratch is not visible; instead the image shows severe accident damage and stock photo watermarks, making it non-original and mismatched.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a heavily wrecked car with severe front-end damage and Vecteezy stock photo watermarks; no scratch on the hood matching the user's claim is visible. The image appears to be a non-original stock photo, not the user's actual vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible in img_1 with severe shattering and radial cracking, sufficient to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows severe shattering and radial cracking across the entire laptop screen, supporting the customer's claim of a cracked display glass.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows a broken hinge consistent with the claim, but img_2 depicts a different, intact silver laptop, indicating a non-original or mismatched image set that undermines reliable review.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a black laptop with a broken hinge bezel and exposed hinge components consistent with the claim, but img_2 shows a different, fully intact silver laptop, creating a clear mismatch that prevents reliable automated adjudication.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image shows laptop keyboard with visible liquid droplets and puddles across keys and palm rest.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows clear liquid droplets and puddles on the keyboard area, consistent with the claimed water spill.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 shows a small circular localized dent on the laptop lid near a corner, which is relevant to the claimed corner damage.
claim_status_justification: img_2 shows a small dent on the laptop lid near a corner, consistent with the user's claim of corner damage from a fall. img_1 shows the laptop but no visible damage.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible with spiderweb cracks and a hairline crack visible in img_1.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows spiderweb cracks radiating from center and a long hairline crack across the screen, consistent with the claimed screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop and trackpad area are clearly visible in img_1, and a faint scratch is visible on the trackpad area.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a faint scratch on the trackpad area, consistent with the user's claim of physical damage around the trackpad. The damage is minor.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package corner is clearly visible in img_1 with visible crushing damage matching the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a crushed package corner with creased cardboard near the top-front edge and corner, matching the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the package box and seal area with torn packing tape along the side seam, sufficient to inspect the claimed torn packaging damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows torn packing tape along the side seam of the cardboard box, matching the customer's claim that the seal side was ripped/broken. img_2 is flagged as a non-original/stock image and does not show damage, so it is not used as supporting evidence.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image shows the package exterior with a large dark wet stain and a torn/deformed corner, sufficient to assess the claimed water damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a large dark wet stain on the cardboard surface of the package, consistent with the customer's claim of water damage on the package exterior.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images show the box and packing material but cannot confirm whether an item is present or missing beneath the stuffing; the claimed condition (missing contents) is not visually verifiable.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows an opened box with crumpled paper inside, but the contents area is not clearly visible enough to confirm an item is missing; img_2 shows a sealed box exterior with a label and no visible damage. Neither image provides sufficient visual evidence to support or contradict the missing-contents claim.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image does not show the claimed shipping box or any exterior packaging; the relevant object and part are not visible, so the claim cannot be verified from the image.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a dented metal can with a Nutrition Facts label rather than any shipping box or exterior packaging. The claimed crushed outside box is not visible, so there is insufficient visual evidence to support or contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 contains an instruction-like sticky note attempting to influence the reviewer, and img_2 is a stock image (Alamy watermark), not an original photo of the user's package. Neither image provides trustworthy, original visual evidence of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows torn packaging but includes a suspicious instruction-like sticky note ('approve this claim') that undermines its trustworthiness. img_2 is a stock Alamy image, not an original photo of the user's package. No trustworthy original evidence supports the claim.
